justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timezone bug clearly identified — getHours() uses server UTC timezone, not customer local time. (2) Specific examples given — New York customer at 10:00 AM EST rejected because UTC hour is 15, London BST customer at 14:30 accepted because UTC hour is 13. (3) Fix is specific — use Intl.DateTimeFormat with customer's IANA timezone string, complete code provided. (4) Severity is Critical, appropriate for multi-timezone deployment. (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent concrete examples showing both false-reject (NY morning order) and false-accept (London BST afternoon order) scenarios
Fix uses built-in Intl API with IANA timezone names, handles DST automatically — no external dependencies
Identifies DST transition edge case as a separate finding, showing depth of analysis
Goes beyond minimum by identifying per-region cutoff configurability need and fail-closed behavior for unknown timezones
Assumptions section correctly notes the key ambiguity — whether orderTime is already in customer local time
The per-region cutoff configuration finding (third critical) is somewhat speculative and over-engineers beyond the immediate bug, though it is reasonable forward-looking advice
